product_overview: Hello Staff is a multi-role hospitality hiring SaaS focused on backend reliability, authorization, validation, and security across candidate, employer, and admin workflows. The system supports job discovery, applications, dashboards, and admin oversight, with regression testing centered on preventing data leaks and broken business logic.
core_goals: Verify secure authentication and session behavior using the provided Basic Auth test account.
Validate role-based authorization for candidate, employer owner, employer manager, and platform admin flows.
Ensure CRUD operations for users, profiles, jobs, applications, and admin-managed content are consistent and do not corrupt data.
Confirm dashboards and list views return only scoped data for the authenticated user or role.
Detect validation, error handling, and security issues including malformed input, unauthorized access, and sensitive data exposure.

features.name: Authentication and Session Management
features.description: Validate login behavior, protected access, and safe handling of invalid credentials or missing authentication.
user_flows: GET / with Basic Auth credentials -> Receive 200 and confirm authenticated access to the backend -> GET /admin/users with valid admin session -> Receive 200 and authorized data
GET / with invalid password -> Receive 401 Unauthorized -> GET /admin/users without credentials -> Receive 401 Unauthorized
POST /auth/login with malformed or missing credentials -> Receive 400 Bad Request with validation error details -> No session or token is issued

features.name: User Registration and Profile Management
features.description: Support creation and maintenance of candidate, employer, manager, and admin-like profiles where permitted, with validation of required fields and role boundaries.
user_flows: POST /api/users or equivalent registration endpoint with valid candidate profile data -> Receive 201 Created -> GET /api/profile with authenticated user -> Receive profile data
POST /api/users with duplicate email or invalid role/email format -> Receive 409 Conflict or 400 Bad Request -> No user record is created
PATCH /api/profile with unauthorized field changes or role escalation attempt -> Receive 403 Forbidden or 400 Bad Request -> Existing role and profile data remain unchanged

features.name: Job Posting and Job Browsing
features.description: Allow employers to create and manage jobs while candidates can browse and view active listings, with strict ownership and validation rules.
user_flows: POST /api/jobs with employer credentials and valid job payload -> Receive 201 Created -> GET /api/jobs -> Receive list of active jobs
POST /api/jobs with candidate credentials -> Receive 403 Forbidden -> No job is created
PATCH /api/jobs/{id} with another employer's job id -> Receive 403 Forbidden or 404 Not Found -> Job remains unchanged
POST /api/jobs with missing fields or invalid enum values -> Receive 400 Bad Request -> No job is stored

features.name: Application Workflow
features.description: Support candidate applications to active jobs, duplicate prevention, employer review, and candidate status visibility.
user_flows: POST /api/applications with candidate credentials and a valid active job id -> Receive 201 Created -> GET /api/applications/{id} -> Receive application status
POST /api/applications with the same candidate and job id again -> Receive 409 Conflict -> Existing application is unchanged
GET /api/employer/applicants for an employer's own job -> Receive 200 and applicant list -> PATCH /api/applications/{id} with decision payload -> Receive 200 with updated status
GET /api/applications/{id} for another user's application -> Receive 403 Forbidden or 404 Not Found -> No private application data is exposed

features.name: Dashboard and Analytics
features.description: Return role-scoped dashboard data for candidates, employers, and admins without cross-tenant leakage.
user_flows: GET /api/dashboard/candidate with candidate credentials -> Receive 200 and scoped dashboard metrics -> GET /api/dashboard/candidate with saved jobs and applications visible
GET /api/dashboard/employer with employer credentials -> Receive 200 and employer-specific jobs, applicants, and activity
GET /api/dashboard/admin with non-admin credentials -> Receive 403 Forbidden -> No platform-level metrics are returned
GET /api/dashboard/admin with admin credentials -> Receive 200 and platform metrics only

features.name: Admin and Moderation
features.description: Provide admin-only access to list users, manage subscriptions, and moderate platform content.
user_flows: GET /admin/users with admin credentials -> Receive 200 and user list -> GET /admin/subscriptions -> Receive 200 and subscription data
GET /admin/users with candidate or employer credentials -> Receive 403 Forbidden -> No user list is returned
POST /api/subscriptions/upgrade with admin credentials and valid target user payload -> Receive 200 or 201 with updated plan -> GET /admin/subscriptions shows the upgraded subscription
POST /api/articles with admin credentials and valid article payload -> Receive 201 Created -> GET /admin/articles returns the new article
POST /api/articles with missing title or unauthorized credentials -> Receive 400 Bad Request or 403 Forbidden -> No article is created

features.name: Articles Management and Public Blog
features.description: Enable admins to create and list articles while public users can read individual blog posts by slug.
user_flows: GET /blog/[slug] with a valid published slug -> Receive 200 and article content -> GET /admin/articles with admin credentials confirms the article exists
GET /blog/[slug] with an unknown slug -> Receive 404 Not Found -> No article content is returned
POST /api/articles with admin credentials and valid content -> Receive 201 Created -> GET /blog/[slug] renders the published article for public access

features.name: Subscriptions Management
features.description: Admin dashboard to manage user subscriptions, plans, and payments.

features.name: User Management
features.description: Admin interface to view, delete, and change roles for platform users.

features.name: Articles Management
features.description: Content management system for platform articles and blog posts.

features.name: Blog
features.description: Public facing blog to read articles.

known_limitations.issue: No server-side validation for admin role on API routes
known_limitations.impact: Security vulnerability if API endpoints are called directly
